recent_calls_14d.subject: Abstimmung BMi/Zycus
recent_calls_14d.snippet:
1: Hallo Petra.
Hallo.
1: Ich hab's doch zeitlich geschafft. Ich hab kurz eine E-mail geschrieben. Ich saß bis grad nachm Taxi. Okay. Hab ich gar nicht gesehen. Alles gut. Super. Passt. Weil Okay. Der Flieger, ich war in München auf Fahrschaffung gestern und der Flieger war heute Morgen etwas zu spät.
Okay, okay. Passt, bin da. Ihr seid auch viel unterwegs, oder im Moment? Das ist absolut Wahnsinn.
1: Ja, ja. Und wenn wir nicht bei Kunden sind, dann hat Marketing eine gute Veranstaltung für uns. Gestern Abend war's auch tatsächlich gut. Das war in München, das war der son CPO Circle. Okay. Und das war superinteressant. Also es war ging gar nicht. Sales Pitch und und Ähnliches, war einfach nur zum Zuhören, zum Lernen, zum zu gucken, wo wo gehen die Strömungen hin? Es war sehr gut. Das war in München im Les Meridians, da auf dem Bahnhof. Das fängt. Fing. Drei Uhr an, ging bis abends. Halb zehn. So das das war war war gut. Aber halt dann 'n langer langer Abend und dann wieder heute Morgen früh los, aber alles gut. Das Wochenende kommt jetzt nach. Das Wochenende So ist.
Ist es. So ist es. Ja. Ich mach mal das Ding auf. Also danke auf alle Fälle mit dem für das Assessment, mit dem Business Case.
Ich weiß nicht, wie wollen wir's durchgehen?
1: Also heute ist ja noch, also der Punkt ist, durchgehen wollten wir ja nächste Woche mit dem Kollegen. Heute war ja erst mal, Du hast ja mit zurückgeschrieben, dass Du gemerkt hast, oh, kommerziell kommen wir so gar nicht weiter. Und da wollte

our_open_deliverables.text: Send RFP/maturity assessment questionnaire and business case template to Petra immediately after 11 Jun call.
Prepare parallel cost/investment estimate for proposed modules (iAnalyze, iSupplier, iContract, iSource, eProcurement) so business case includes both savings and investment.
Complete first-cut spend analysis on BMI's finalized data and prepare findings to present at 8 Jul workshop.
Send reply to BMI's maturity and business case information.
Develop visual process flow diagram showing integration between ERP (Guss), Zycus platform, and other tools (Atlassian, warehouse systems) with document types and data flows.
Deliver finalized business case with quantified ROI and savings model for Petra to present to board.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Send finalized spend data extract (Excel) to Zycus to enable business case calculations.
Complete and return maturity assessment questionnaire to Zycus.
Provide missing data from finance/controlling via IT ticket to complete spend dataset.

competitive_position.summary: BMI is running a head-to-head competitive evaluation between Zycus and one unnamed second supplier, with both presenting to the two-shareholder board on 20 Jul 2026 for a final decision. Zycus has a strong process advantage: onsite workshop completed 5 May, maturity assessment and integration architecture underway, and Petra (CPO, Decision Maker, champion) is actively building the internal case for Zycus by requesting business case materials and driving the timeline. However, Zycus is competing blind—Petra has not disclosed the competitor's identity, positioning, or pricing—so the wedge is unclear and competitive objections cannot be pre-empted. The risk is that the competitor delivers a stronger business case, undercuts on price, or has a feature/relationship advantage (e.g., better Guss ERP integration, dairy cooperative sector specialization) that Zycus does not see coming. Sentiment is neutral to positive (Petra is engaged and advocating), but the parallel evaluation and lack of competitive intelligence keep the position exposed.

competitors.name: Second supplier (unnamed)
competitors.quote: We are also evaluating one other supplier. Both of you will present to the board on 20 July and the board will decide.

champion_strength.source: Avoma discovery call 11 Jun 2026, Petra Dörr; SF OpportunityContactRole; SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026; SF LastActivityDate 16 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: Petra Dörr (CPO, Decision Maker) is a strong and active champion with clear mandate, board access, and advocacy. She is driving the evaluation timeline (set 8 Jul workshop and 20 Jul board decision), building the internal case for Zycus (requested business case, maturity assessment, integration diagram), and explicitly stated 'I will present the business case to our board on 20 July.' However, her engagement is recent (last call 11 Jun, 18 days ago; last activity 16 Jun, 13 days ago) and the Next Step (22 Jun) shows Zycus owes a reply by 26 Jun (now 3 days overdue as of 29 Jun). If Zycus does not deliver promptly, Petra's advocacy could weaken or signal that Zycus cannot execute at the required speed. Champion strength is strong but at risk due to execution lag.

sources: Avoma discovery call 11 Jun 2026, Petra Dörr
Known risk: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged; the two-shareholder board holds budget authority but no individual EB contact is named, mapped, or has validated sponsorship.
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is the two-shareholder board of Bayerische Milchindustrie eG, which holds final budget authority for platform investments. On the 11 Jun call Petra stated 'I will present the business case to our board on 20 July, and they will make the decision. We have two shareholders who sit on the board.' Petra (CPO, Decision Maker) controls the evaluation process and will present the recommendation, but the commercial and budget approval sits with the board. However, no individual Economic Buyer contact on that board has been identified, mapped, or engaged. The evidence shows that Petra has access to the board and is driving the agenda, but Zycus has no direct relationship with either shareholder or the managing director who would validate sponsorship, confirm budget availability, or pre-align on the investment before the 20 Jul presentation. The Next Step references 'John to arrange a call with CPO to talk about the scope and agenda for the onsite meeting' (Apr 14) and mentions 'John, Monika and Dirk' (Zycus team members) but no board-level contact. If the board raises an objection Petra cannot address, or if budget authority is ambiguous, the decision could delay or fail despite Petra's strong advocacy. Identifying and engaging at least one board member or the managing director before 20 Jul is the critical gap.

recommended_moves: Deliver the overdue reply to BMI immediately (due 26 Jun, now 3 days late) and confirm receipt of BMI's spend data. If the data has not arrived, escalate directly to Petra to secure it within 48 hours, emphasizing that without it the 8 Jul workshop ROI story will be incomplete. If the data has arrived, fast-track the spend analysis to present quantified savings at the 8 Jul workshop.

Multi-thread into BMI's finance lead and at least one board member before the 8 Jul workshop. Ask Petra directly: 'To make sure the board presentation on 20 Jul addresses all their concerns, can you introduce us to the Finanzchef (finance lead) and one of the two shareholders who will be in the room? We want to understand their priorities and pre-align on the ROI story.' Position this as de-risking HER presentation, not bypassing her.

Extract competitive intelligence from Petra before the 8 Jul workshop. Ask directly: 'You mentioned on 11 Jun that one other supplier will also present to the board on 20 Jul. To make sure our presentation addresses all the decision criteria, can you share what the other supplier is leading with—their pricing model, key differentiators, or any concerns you have about their solution?' Use this to tailor the 8 Jul workshop and board presentation to pre-empt competitive objections.
